The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), through its groundbreaking youth initiative YuWaah, is seeking applications for the position of Meaningful Youth Participation Specialist based in New Delhi, India. This prestigious National United Nations Volunteer (UNV) assignment presents an exceptional opportunity for experienced youth engagement professionals to contribute to shaping the future of millions of young […]

The post UNICEF Meaningful Youth Participation Specialist in India 2026: National UN Volunteer Opportunity in New Delhi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), through its groundbreaking youth initiative YuWaah, is seeking applications for the position of Meaningful Youth Participation Specialist based in New Delhi, India. This prestigious National United Nations Volunteer (UNV) assignment presents an exceptional opportunity for experienced youth engagement professionals to contribute to shaping the future of millions of young people across India.

The assignment offers a 12-month full-time placement with UNICEF and the YuWaah Secretariat, providing volunteers with the opportunity to work directly with government institutions, private sector partners, civil society organizations, and youth networks to advance meaningful youth participation and youth-centric development across the country.

The application deadline is 1 July 2026.

About UNICEF’s Mission

UNICEF operates under a fundamental mission to promote and protect the rights of every child, everywhere. The organization works tirelessly to ensure that children and adolescents, especially the most disadvantaged and marginalized, have equal opportunities to survive, develop, and reach their full potential.

At the heart of UNICEF’s work lies a commitment to equity. The organization recognizes that when any child faces unequal opportunities in social, political, economic, civic, or cultural dimensions, their rights are compromised. UNICEF believes that investing in disadvantaged children and young people not only improves individual outcomes but also contributes to sustained national growth, stability, and development.

This commitment is reflected in UNICEF’s global efforts to empower adolescents and youth through innovative programs, partnerships, and policy initiatives.

Understanding YuWaah and Generation Unlimited

In recent years, UNICEF has significantly expanded its focus on adolescents and young people as global challenges affecting youth have become increasingly apparent.

To address these challenges, UNICEF launched Generation Unlimited (GenU), a global partnership dedicated to creating opportunities for young people aged 10 to 24 years. Generation Unlimited brings together:

  • United Nations agencies
  • Governments
  • Civil society organizations
  • Private sector partners
  • Young people themselves

The overarching goal is to ensure that by 2030 every young person is:

  • In school
  • Engaged in learning
  • Receiving vocational training
  • Employed
  • Building entrepreneurial opportunities

What is YuWaah?

YuWaah is India’s adaptation of Generation Unlimited.

YuWaah serves as a catalytic partnership platform designed to unlock the immense potential of India’s adolescents and youth by creating scalable, innovative solutions that:

  • Improve learning opportunities
  • Expand vocational and functional training access
  • Promote employability
  • Encourage entrepreneurship
  • Foster youth leadership
  • Strengthen youth participation in decision-making

Given that young people represent a substantial proportion of India’s population, YuWaah recognizes their right to influence policies, programs, investments, and decisions that affect their lives.

Why Youth Participation Matters

Research consistently demonstrates the value of investing in youth. According to evidence referenced by UNICEF, every additional year of secondary education significantly increases lifetime earnings and contributes to broader societal development.

Meaningful youth participation ensures that:

  • Policies reflect real youth needs.
  • Programs remain relevant and responsive.
  • Public investments generate stronger outcomes.
  • Organizations become more inclusive.
  • Governments develop more effective interventions.
  • Companies build stronger youth-centered workplace cultures.

By integrating young people’s voices directly into decision-making processes, institutions can design solutions that better reflect lived realities and maximize impact.

Position Overview: Meaningful Youth Participation Specialist

The selected UN Volunteer will be stationed in New Delhi and work closely with UNICEF and the YuWaah Secretariat.

The specialist will play a critical role in designing, coordinating, and operationalizing Yutopia: A Public-Private-Youth Collective, a nationwide alliance focused on embedding youth centrality across India’s public and private sector systems.

The role involves fostering collaboration among:

  • Government ministries
  • Private sector companies
  • Civil society organizations
  • Youth networks
  • Development partners
  • Community stakeholders

The specialist will support the integration of youth perspectives into policy development, organizational practices, and program implementation.

Key Responsibilities

The selected volunteer will undertake a broad range of responsibilities, including:

1. Supporting Youth Engagement Strategies

  • Implement UNICEF-YuWaah youth engagement priorities.
  • Coordinate activities aligned with FY 2026–2027 plans.
  • Work closely with multiple programme teams.

2. Managing the Young People’s Action Team (YPAT)

The specialist will support the fifth cohort of YuWaah’s Young People’s Action Team by:

  • Coordinating engagement activities
  • Organizing meetings
  • Facilitating capacity-building sessions
  • Supporting town halls
  • Arranging interactions with leadership stakeholders

3. Strengthening Youth Mentorship Opportunities

Responsibilities include:

  • Coordinating mentorship initiatives
  • Engaging YuWaah Governing Board members
  • Facilitating leadership interactions
  • Creating opportunities for youth development

4. Building Strategic Partnerships

The volunteer will:

  • Draft briefing notes
  • Prepare stakeholder engagement plans
  • Coordinate partnerships with government agencies
  • Engage civil society organizations
  • Collaborate with private sector actors

5. Supporting Youth-Centric Standards and Benchmarks

The assignment includes helping establish standards for organizations interested in adopting youth-centered approaches and practices.

6. Supporting the Yutopia Alliance

Key activities include:

  • Promoting alliance initiatives
  • Amplifying partner commitments
  • Facilitating knowledge sharing
  • Supporting collaboration among stakeholders

7. Communications and Social Media

The specialist will assist in:

  • Youth engagement campaigns
  • Strategic communications
  • Social media outreach
  • Awareness initiatives
  • Public engagement efforts

Expected Deliverables

The selected volunteer is expected to contribute to several high-impact outputs, including:

Strategic Documents

  • YuWaah youth engagement strategy note
  • Fifth cohort YPAT strategy framework
  • International Youth Day 2026 campaign strategy
  • FY 2026–27 youth engagement social media strategy

Knowledge Products

  • Youth-friendly workplace benchmark report
  • Evidence-based youth engagement resources
  • Stakeholder learning products
  • Best-practice documentation

Media and Communication Outputs

  • Creative audio-visual materials
  • Yutopia Alliance impact stories
  • Youth-centered communication products
  • Knowledge dissemination materials

Required Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

Educational Qualification

A Bachelor’s Degree in:

  • Development Studies
  • Social Sciences
  • Humanities
  • Social Work
  • Related disciplines

Experience

At least 3 years of experience in:

  • Youth engagement
  • Programme design and implementation
  • Participatory approaches
  • Governance and policy engagement
  • Development programming
  • Stakeholder coordination

Language Requirements

Applicants must be fluent in:

  • English
  • Hindi

Essential Skills and Competencies

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Technical Skills

  • Programme coordination
  • Youth engagement methodologies
  • Participatory facilitation
  • Policy advocacy
  • Strategic communication
  • Report writing
  • Stakeholder engagement

Professional Competencies

  • Leadership
  • Teamwork
  • Integrity
  • Professionalism
  • Adaptability
  • Cultural sensitivity
  • Planning and organization
  • Continuous learning

Specialized Knowledge

  • Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  • Inclusion and equity principles
  • Youth participation frameworks
  • Gender-responsive programming
  • Community engagement approaches

Benefits and Entitlements

The selected National UN Volunteer will receive a comprehensive benefits package.

Financial Benefits

Monthly Volunteer Living Allowance

  • INR 70,158.41 per month

Entry Lump Sum

  • USD 350 equivalent in INR

Relocation Support

  • USD 175 at the beginning and end of the assignment (where applicable)

Exit Allowance

  • USD 120 per month served
  • Paid upon successful completion of the assignment

Insurance Coverage

Comprehensive insurance including:

  • Health insurance
  • Life insurance
  • Disability insurance
  • Malicious acts coverage

Leave Entitlements

  • 2.5 days annual leave per month

Professional Development

  • Access to UNV learning resources
  • Career development opportunities
  • Professional training support

Living and Working in New Delhi

The assignment is based at UNICEF’s office in New Delhi, India’s vibrant capital city.

New Delhi offers:

  • Diverse cultural experiences
  • Rich historical heritage
  • Extensive professional networking opportunities
  • Exposure to international development initiatives
  • Access to major governmental institutions

As a national assignment, volunteers are responsible for arranging their own housing and living arrangements.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The United Nations Volunteers programme is committed to equal opportunity and welcomes applications from qualified professionals regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Age
  • Disability
  • Religion
  • Ethnicity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Social background
  • National origin

UNICEF also provides reasonable accommodations for volunteers with disabilities to ensure equal participation throughout the recruitment and assignment process.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity offers a unique platform to:

  • Influence youth development policies at national scale.
  • Collaborate with leading institutions and policymakers.
  • Contribute to UNICEF’s mission.
  • Strengthen youth participation systems.
  • Build expertise in international development.
  • Expand professional networks.
  • Support India’s youth empowerment agenda.

For professionals passionate about youth engagement, policy influence, social development, and inclusive governance, this assignment provides an exceptional opportunity to create lasting impact.

Application Deadline

1 July 2026

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Volunteer Position opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post UNICEF Meaningful Youth Participation Specialist in India 2026: National UN Volunteer Opportunity in New Delhi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland: Receive €25,000 Annual Stipend Plus Full Tuition for Four Years
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Are you passionate about public policy, climate action, social justice, housing, governance, or interdisciplinary research? Dublin City University (DCU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD scholarship at the School of Law and Government, offering an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers from Ireland and around the world to pursue doctoral studies in one of […]

The post Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland: Receive €25,000 Annual Stipend Plus Full Tuition for Four Years appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about public policy, climate action, social justice, housing, governance, or interdisciplinary research? Dublin City University (DCU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD scholarship at the School of Law and Government, offering an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers from Ireland and around the world to pursue doctoral studies in one of Ireland’s leading universities.

The successful candidate will join the SEAI-funded SHINE research project, a four-year interdisciplinary initiative exploring how social housing residents engage with clean energy transitions, climate action, and technological innovation. The scholarship provides a generous annual stipend of €25,000, full tuition coverage (up to the non-EU fee rate), expert supervision, and the opportunity to contribute to impactful research that addresses energy poverty and supports a just transition toward a low-carbon future.

Applications are open now, with the deadline set for 8 July 2026 at 5:00 PM.

About the DCU Fully Funded PhD Scholarship

The School of Law and Government at Dublin City University is offering a fully funded doctoral scholarship for students interested in interdisciplinary research that combines public policy, governance, technology, climate change, and social justice.

The PhD scholarship forms part of the SHINE (Supporting Housing through Intelligent and Networked Energy) research project, funded by the Sustainable Energy Authority of Ireland (SEAI). The project is jointly led by Dr. Valesca Lima from the School of Law and Government and Professor Stephen Daniels from the School of Electronic Engineering.

Over four years, SHINE will investigate how citizen science can help improve understanding of the relationship between social housing, clean energy, climate resilience, and policy development.

The successful PhD researcher will be based in Dublin, Ireland, throughout the duration of the programme and will become part of a collaborative research team working across multiple academic disciplines.

About the SHINE Research Project

The SHINE project aims to address one of today’s most pressing societal challenges—ensuring that the transition to clean energy is both fair and inclusive.

The project recognizes that national climate ambitions cannot be achieved without effectively supporting vulnerable households, particularly those living in social housing.

Its research focuses on developing intelligent and evidence-based solutions that can:

  • Address energy poverty.
  • Improve climate resilience.
  • Support behavioural change.
  • Promote citizen engagement.
  • Encourage a just transition.
  • Integrate emerging technologies into housing policy.

Through citizen science and interdisciplinary collaboration, SHINE seeks to generate knowledge that informs national climate and housing strategies while improving the well-being of communities.

PhD Research Focus

Applicants are encouraged to design their own doctoral research project within the wider SHINE framework.

The university welcomes innovative research proposals that embrace the interdisciplinary nature of the project while addressing challenges related to:

  • Public policy.
  • Housing.
  • Climate governance.
  • Citizen engagement.
  • Technology adoption.
  • Energy systems.
  • Social justice.
  • Policy innovation.

The research should contribute meaningful insights into how governance, technology, and citizen participation can help achieve fair and sustainable climate transitions.

Indicative Research Themes

Potential research topics include:

  • Socio-technical policy and governance.
  • Human-technology interaction.
  • Human-data interaction.
  • Citizen science in housing policy.
  • National housing strategies.
  • Energy literacy.
  • Household behavioural change.
  • Climate action planning.
  • Public policy frameworks.
  • Housing regulations.
  • Just transition governance.
  • Policy innovation.

Applicants are encouraged to develop original research questions that align with these themes while demonstrating creativity and interdisciplinary thinking.

Why You Should Apply

The DCU PhD Scholarship offers much more than financial support.

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Full funding for four years.
  • Annual stipend of €25,000.
  • Tuition fees covered up to the non-EU rate.
  • Opportunity to conduct impactful interdisciplinary research.
  • Supervision by experienced academic experts.
  • Membership in a supportive research community.
  • Skills development and professional training.
  • Freedom to shape an independent doctoral research project.
  • Collaboration across public policy and engineering disciplines.
  • Contribution to research that influences climate and housing policy.

Research Environment at Dublin City University

Successful candidates will join one of Ireland’s most dynamic research environments.

DCU provides:

  • Dedicated academic supervision.
  • Collaborative interdisciplinary teams.
  • Opportunities for professional development.
  • Research training.
  • Academic networking.
  • Support in developing independent research careers.

Students are encouraged to pursue innovative ideas while benefiting from guidance from experienced supervisors.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should possess a strong academic background and an interest in interdisciplinary research.

Essential Qualification

Applicants should hold a Level 8 Bachelor’s Degree in one or more relevant disciplines, including:

  • Political Science.
  • Public Policy.
  • Sociology.
  • Human Geography.
  • Education.
  • Data Science.
  • Environmental Science.
  • Sustainable Systems.
  • Other engineering or environmental fields with a demonstrated interest in social justice.

Desirable Qualification

Applicants with a Level 9 Master’s Degree in a relevant field are particularly encouraged to apply.

Although a master’s degree is desirable, applicants should carefully review the official requirements to determine their eligibility.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are welcomed from:

  • Applicants from Ireland.
  • Applicants from European Union countries.
  • International applicants from around the world.

Successful candidates will be expected to relocate to Dublin and remain based there for the duration of the four-year doctoral programme.

Applicants shortlisted for the position will participate in an online interview during mid-July.

Applicants may also be required to demonstrate English language proficiency where applicable.

Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (maximum three pages).
  2. Motivation Letter (maximum two pages).

Applications should clearly demonstrate:

  • Academic preparation.
  • Research interests.
  • Motivation for pursuing the PhD.
  • Interest in interdisciplinary research.
  • Alignment with the SHINE project objectives.

How to Apply

Completed applications should be submitted via email to the address provided by Dublin City University.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare high-quality application materials that demonstrate their research potential, interdisciplinary interests, and commitment to addressing climate, housing, and social justice challenges.

Informal enquiries regarding the position may be directed to Dr. Valesca Lima.

Application Deadline

Applications close on 8 July 2026 at 5:00 PM.

Late applications may not be considered, so prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their documents well before the deadline.

Apply: send application to tracymae.ildefonso@dcu.ie 

Scholarship Summary

Item Details
Institution Dublin City University (DCU)
School School of Law and Government
Country Ireland
Funding Fully Funded
Annual Stipend €25,000
Tuition Covered up to the non-EU fee rate
Duration Four years
Project SHINE (SEAI-funded)
Start Date September 2026
Deadline 8 July 2026, 5:00 PM

Final Thoughts

The Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers who want to contribute to climate policy, social justice, energy transitions, and public governance. By joining the SHINE research project, doctoral candidates will work on meaningful interdisciplinary research with real-world policy implications while receiving comprehensive financial support throughout their studies.

If you are passionate about addressing energy poverty, promoting sustainable housing policies, and shaping equitable climate solutions, this fully funded PhD programme at Dublin City University could be the ideal next step in your academic and professional journey. Be sure to submit your application before the 8 July 2026, 5:00 PM deadline.

For more information, visit the official call for applications

Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland: Receive €25,000 Annual Stipend Plus Full Tuition for Four Years appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
